reasoning: The question asks if the Fed will execute a "cut-pause-pause" sequence across the September, October, and December 2025 FOMC meetings. The September 16-17, 2025 meeting concluded with a 25 bps rate cut, satisfying the first condition ("cut"). However, as of late October 2025, market pricing (e.g., CME FedWatch) indicates a ~99% probability of another 25 bps rate cut at the upcoming October 28-29 meeting. If the Fed cuts rates in October, the sequence will be "cut-cut," which immediately renders the "cut-pause-pause" condition impossible. Given that the probability of a "pause" in October is around 1% (and even if it did occur, a subsequent pause in December is not guaranteed), the overall probability of a "cut-pause-pause" sequence is extremely low. I am submitting 0.02, as it represents the minimum prediction threshold for this extreme unlikelihood.
